note: Each entry names a guard and the mutation that removes it. guard-check applies the mutation and requires the named test to go RED. A surviving mutation means the guard has no proof, which is the condition the rc.6 audit found twice and this registry exists to make a machine judgement. expectedMatches is also the drift detector: if a guard gains or loses a site, the count stops matching and the entry fails before the mutation is applied.

guards.rationale: integerValue is the single arbiter of integer-ness for the knowledge pagination flags. Bare Number() sent NaN into core, which reported a typo as a range refusal.
Same arbiter on the sibling flag. Held separately because a fix that covered only one of the pair would otherwise pass.
A truthy guard cannot tell an empty flag value from an absent one, and swallowed --segment-events= entirely.
migration-plan --target-version abc answered WORKSPACE_MIGRATION_DOWNGRADE with the message NaN: a syntax error dressed as a judgement about versions.
Same class on the knowledge marker verbs, which reported the NaN as a promotion-version refusal without naming the flag.

includes(String(x)) admits a single-element array, a toString object, and a boxed String onto a legal enum member. This is the site that required an Owner exemption to Constraint 1. The mutation restores the exact pre-fix text from 6cf5c70 rather than simply deleting the typeof check: deleting it leaves governedAction as unknown and the pinned compiler rejects the file, which would prove the compiler bites and say nothing about whether the test does.
extension-registration appliesTo admitted [["work"],["work"]] through both the membership test and the duplicate test, because Set dedupe is identity-based.

Batching the privacy scan introduced an explicit maxBuffer, and an overflow there had to stay distinguishable from a command that simply failed. A blanket result.error check would reclassify ENOENT and ETIMEDOUT.

The mutation claim is released at every non-crash escape. The crash point keeps the claim on purpose, because a real SIGKILL never runs a finally and the retained claim is what marks the store mid-write.
guards.testName: WSC-2: knowledge-rebase enforces CAS and fails closed at fault points leaving the store locked

guards.rationale: The quarantine name must start with a prefix the residue gate recognises. stale-recovery- matched neither RESIDUE_PREFIX nor .gitignore, so a leaked quarantine file was invisible to the residue gate, entered the backup manifest, and could be committed and shipped to every cloner.
guards.testName: WSA-7b: a reclaimed recovery claim quarantines under a residue-visible prefix

guards.rationale: readSourceFile asserts nlink === 1, which is right for tracked source and a category error for a package manager's tree: pnpm hardlinks from a content-addressable store, so every CI runner failed SOURCE_HARDLINK while a cross-filesystem local store stayed green. The relaxation is scoped to a caller that names it; this entry pins that removing the scope reddens.

The hardlink relaxation is scoped to a package manager tree. As first written that scope was a comment, so a later caller could aim the reader at a tracked source file and drop the guard for that read with nothing to say so -- documented, not enforced, in the very function added to fix a misapplied guard.

The ppid===1 orphan-pattern branch catches a leaked load whose process-group leader already exited and whose pgid is a defunct group we never see registered live — the exact 2026-07-24 shape (35 yes orphans reparented to init). Without it, that class of orphan goes undetected.
guards.testName: BR: the orphan-pattern backstop catches orphans whose group was never registered

guards.rationale: The registered-group branch reports any process still alive in a group the session declared it owns, including non-orphan members whose leader is still running (a dev server left behind). Without it, only reparented orphans are caught and a still-parented leak escapes.
guards.testName: BR: a registered group with a live non-orphan member is residue
